title: Crisis Email Blast Size to All Employees

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST CSF RC.CO-01 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST CSF RC.CO-01 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.7.49: Crisis Email Blast Size to All Employees.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.7.49' running on ms:o365:message_trace, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=o365 sourcetype=ms:o365:message_trace earliest=-90d
| stats count as msgs by SenderAddress, bin(_time,1h)
| where msgs>5000
description: Detects unusual internal blast emails that may indicate crisis communication during recovery.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST CSF compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes o365 and sourcetypes ms:o365:message_trace. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
grandmaExplanation: We keep evidence for Crisis Email Blast Size to All Employees. The NIST Cybersecurity Framework tells us what good practice looks like, and we keep evidence we match it.
implementation: (1) Allowlist known HR broadcasts; (2) align with comms playbook; (3) privacy review. Indexes required: `index=o365`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ms:o365:message_trace`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: O365 message trace.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `ms:o365:message_trace`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Allowlist known HR broadcasts; (2) align with comms playbook; (3) privacy review.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=o365 sourcetype=ms:o365:message_trace earliest=-90d
| stats count as msgs by SenderAddress, bin(_time,1h)
| where msgs>5000
```

Understanding this SPL

**Crisis Email Blast Size to All Employees (RC.CO-01)** — Detects unusual internal blast emails that may indicate crisis communication during recovery.

Documented **Data sources**: `ms:o365:message_trace`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): O365 message trace. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: o365; **sourcetype**: ms:o365:message_trace. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=o365, sourcetype=ms:o365:message_trace, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by SenderAddress, bin(_time,1h)** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Filters the current rows with `where msgs>5000` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Table
knownFalsePositives: Recovery time variance from incident complexity, dependency cascades, or limited spare capacity.
